signals.title: AUD/USD — Trump Tariff Escalation
signals.text: AUD COT LF long +48.3k (74% long) creates crowded-long unwind risk — AUD/USD closed 0.7208 Friday; RBA hike bias (0% priced) sustains the bid but any disappointment triggers the unwind. The unwind mechanism is driven by the potential disappointment from the RBA meeting. The catalyst for this setup is the next RBA meeting on 5 May.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Trump Tariff Escalation
signals.text: EUR/USD closed Friday at 1.1723; Trump tariff escalation on EU autos sets up downside pressure toward 1.16 — EUR COT short -18.3k reinforces the bias. The downside pressure is driven by the tariff escalation, which may weaken the euro. The catalyst for this setup is the upcoming ECB meeting on 30 Apr.
evidence: Trump tariff escalation on EU autos

signals.title: JPY/USD — G8 Bilateral Theme + Yield Advantage
signals.text: JPY 1W avg +1.081% (n=7) outperforms USD 1W avg -0.872% (n=7) with a G8 spread of 1.953%, setting up a strong bilateral theme. The yield advantage of USD over JPY by approximately 340bp is likely to sustain the USD bid. The catalyst for this setup is the next BoJ meeting.

signals.title: USD/CHF — Carry Context
signals.text: The USD/CHF pair has a carry advantage of approximately 375bp, with the Fed rate at 3.75% and the SNB rate at 0%. This is likely to sustain the USD bid. The catalyst for this setup is the next SNB meeting on 18 Jun. The CHF/JPY pair has a strong short bias in retail sentiment, which may lead to a contrarian reversal risk.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: With NZD/USD at 0.5898, the -150bp carry differential favors the USD, but specs are short -16.8k contracts, which sets up a short-squeeze risk as the RBNZ's forward bias points to a hike, with 40% of a hike priced in. This divergence creates risk of a sharp unwind, likely to propel NZD/USD higher. The next RBNZ meeting on 27 May is a key catalyst as the key catalyst.

signals.title: USD/JPY — COT vs OIS Divergence + BoJ
signals.text: With the USD/JPY carry differential at +300bp, favoring the USD, and the pair currently trading at 157.03, specs short −88.5k sets up a notable divergence with the BoJ's forward bias, which has a 40% hike priced in. This creates a short-squeeze risk, as the market is positioned against the direction the BoJ is signaling. The unwind of these positions is likely to be catalyzed by the next BoJ meeting on 28 Apr.
